title: Credential Compromise: Merchant Category Diversity Explosion with Clean Transaction Profile
narrative: Fraudster uses stolen card credentials across an unusually broad range of merchant categories (MCCs) within a compressed timeframe, breaking the cardholder's established spending pattern. The card appears simultaneously at retail, digital goods, travel, and service merchants — categories the legitimate holder rarely or never combined. Each individual transaction appears normal: amounts are moderate, billing details verify correctly, and transaction-level velocity stays within acceptable bounds. However, C13 and C14 counters reveal the card is now transacting across 15-24 distinct merchant categories in a 24-hour window, compared to the cardholder's typical 2-4 categories per week. C8 shows the card hitting 20+ merchants in the rolling window despite historical patterns of 3-6 merchants. The fraudster is methodically testing the card's limits across diverse surfaces to maximize extraction before detection, relying on per-transaction normality to avoid basic fraud rules.
indicators: C13 > 24.00 indicating card used across exceptionally high merchant-category count in rolling window
C14 > 7.10 (p90 threshold) showing card active at many more distinct merchant IDs than cardholder baseline
C8 > 29.10 (p90) revealing elevated merchant-diversity counter inconsistent with prior card behavior
C11 between 15.00 and 27.10 showing moderate-to-high card-level transaction count without extreme velocity spikes
card1_distinct_addr1 <= 2 confirming billing address consistency across transactions to mask fraud
TransactionAmt between 60.00 and 200.00 keeping individual amounts within normal cardholder range
C1 < 30.00 showing aggregate card activity elevated but not at extreme p99 levels that trigger basic rules
indicator_explanations: Card suddenly appears across an unusually wide spectrum of merchant categories in short timeframe
Card is active at far more individual merchant locations than the cardholder's typical pattern
Merchant diversity metrics show card being spread across unrelated business types simultaneously
Total transaction count is elevated but not extreme enough to trigger simple velocity alerts
Billing address remains consistent across all transactions to pass address verification checks
Purchase amounts stay within the cardholder's normal spending range on each transaction
Overall card activity is elevated above normal but below the most obvious fraud thresholds
summary: Stolen card credentials are systematically tested across 15-24 diverse merchant categories simultaneously, breaking the cardholder's established shopping pattern while keeping individual transaction amounts and velocity within acceptable ranges to evade basic fraud controls.
reasoning: Legitimate cardholders maintain consistent merchant-category patterns over time, rarely hitting 7-10+ unrelated categories in a single day. The explosion in C13/C14 diversity counters combined with elevated C8 merchant counts reveals coordinated multi-surface testing behavior characteristic of credential-compromise operations maximizing card exploitation before fraud detection triggers account closure.
